name: deny-loop-repeat
doc: Mechanical anti-loop (NEVER propose the same fix twice). Two IDENTICAL denied tool-calls in the same project state dir: the 2nd (and 3rd) get the message rewritten with a `[REPEAT]` title, a `STOP:` prefix and a forced research-expert action, while the DECISION stays block. Uses a tiny `git reset --hard` deny (evaluate GIT_BLOCKED, patterns.ts:9 → block "Destructive git command", evaluate.ts:22-34) as the stable identical block so the payload is small; ANY block is wrapped by withDenyLoop (gate.ts:41, deny-loop-store.ts:79-83). The sidecar deny-loop.json lives in dirname(trackFile) = the per-project state dir, persisting across the separate binary spawns (needs HOME+cwd stable). denyHash keys on tool+normalized input (deny-loop.ts:38), NOT session_id — see 09-session-track-isolation for what IS session-scoped.

steps.doc: First deny is NORMAL (count 1, isRepeat false, deny-loop.ts:50-55). Reason = `[BLOCKED] Destructive git command`.
Identical retry → count 2, isRepeat true → enrichRepeatDeny (deny-loop.ts:65-74): title prefixed `[REPEAT]`.
Still identical → count 3, still repeat; assert the STOP prefix (deny-loop.ts:66 `STOP: ne retente pas ce même appel`).
